entities.name: ball
entities.description: A spherical object used in billiards.

entities.name: cue ball
entities.description: The white ball used to strike other balls in billiards.

entities.name: object ball
entities.description: A colored ball numbered from 1 to 9 used in various games of billiards.

entities.name: 1-cushion billiards
entities.description: A type of billiards where the balls are pocketed using only one cushion.

entities.name: one-pocket
entities.description: A game in which players attempt to pocket all their opponent's balls and their own color group into a single pocket.

entities.name: three-ball
entities.description: A variant of billiards where players use three balls, typically numbered 1, 3, and 5.

entities.name: four-ball billiards
entities.description: A game in which players use four balls, typically numbered 1, 4, 7, and 9.

entities.name: five-pin billiards
entities.description: An Italian-originated game played on a rectangular table with five pins.

entities.name: six-ball
entities.description: A variant of pool where players use six balls, typically numbered 1 through 6.

entities.name: seven-ball
entities.description: A game in which players use seven balls, typically numbered 1 through 7.

entities.name: eight-ball
entities.description: A popular variant of pool where players must pocket all their opponent's balls and their own color group before pocketing the eight ball.

entities.name: nine-ball
entities.description: A game in which players use nine balls, typically numbered 1 through 9.

entities.name: ten-ball
entities.description: A variant of pool where players use ten balls, typically numbered 1 through 10.

entities.name: three-cushion billiards
entities.description: A form of carom billiards where the cue ball must contact both object balls while hitting at least three rail cushions.

entities.name: one-cushion billiards
entities.description: An earlier form of carom billiards that evolved into three-cushion billiards.

entities.name: straight rail billiards
entities.description: A precursor to both one-cushion and three-cushion billiards, which influenced their development.

entities.name: balkline
entities.description: Another carom billiards game that arose from straight rail billiards.

entities.name: cue ball
entities.description: The main ball used in billiards, which players strike with a cue stick to hit other balls on the table.

entities.name: object balls
entities.description: The balls that are struck by the cue ball during gameplay.

entities.name: rail cushions
entities.description: The sides of the billiard table where the cue ball and object balls can hit to score points.
